---
title: "Architecture, Abstraction, and Design"
---

# Architecture, Abstraction, and Design

Range: `0001-0112`.

## Completion

| Status      | Count |
| ----------- | ----- |
| authored    | 21    |
| stub        | 87    |
| needs_sense | 4     |

Use the summary table for scanning. Each row links to the detailed anchored entry below.

| Sigil | Term                                       | Completion  | Summary                                                                                                                                                                                 |
| ----- | ------------------------------------------ | ----------- | --------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------- |
| 0001  | [abstraction](#rune-0001)                  | authored    | A deliberate forgetting of detail so larger structure can be reasoned about without drowning in mechanism. Shadow: hiding the only failure that matters.                                |
| 0002  | [adapter](#rune-0002)                      | authored    | A negotiated translator between unlike shapes. Use it when two systems must meet without infecting one another. Shadow: adapter jungles that preserve confusion rather than resolve it. |
| 0003  | `STUB` [agent {Arch}](#rune-0003)          | stub        | An acting word; a named locus of initiative that may observe, decide, and invoke tools.                                                                                                 |
| 0004  | `STUB` [aggregate {Arch}](#rune-0004)      | stub        | An order-word; it constrains how complexity is arranged rather than merely accumulated.                                                                                                 |
| 0005  | [anti-corruption layer](#rune-0005)        | authored    | A protective seam that keeps one model from polluting another.                                                                                                                          |
| 0006  | [API gateway](#rune-0006)                  | authored    | A threshold that centralizes entry, policy, and routing at the edge of a service world. Shadow: a convenience chokepoint that quietly becomes a monolith.                               |
| 0007  | [architecture](#rune-0007)                 | needs_sense | A world-shaping word; it names the large order into which many smaller decisions must fit.                                                                                              |
| 0008  | `STUB` [artifact boundary](#rune-0008)     | stub        | A composition-word; it lets many smaller parts behave as one larger form.                                                                                                               |
| 0009  | `STUB` [assembler](#rune-0009)             | stub        | A composition-word; it lets many smaller parts behave as one larger form.                                                                                                               |
| 0010  | `STUB` [backbone](#rune-0010)              | stub        | A boundary-word; it shapes how one part may touch another.                                                                                                                              |
| 0011  | `STUB` [backend](#rune-0011)               | stub        | A structure-word; it gives responsibility a place to live.                                                                                                                              |
| 0012  | [boundary](#rune-0012)                     | authored    | The line that says where one responsibility ends and another begins. Shadow: porous edges that smear blame, ownership, and test scope.                                                  |
| 0013  | [bounded context](#rune-0013)              | authored    | A protection against semantic bleed: the same word may not mean the same thing in every subsystem. Shadow: false unification through shared names.                                      |
| 0014  | `STUB` [bridge](#rune-0014)                | stub        | A boundary-word; it shapes how one part may touch another.                                                                                                                              |
| 0015  | `STUB` [broker {Arch}](#rune-0015)         | stub        | A boundary-word; it shapes how one part may touch another.                                                                                                                              |
| 0016  | `STUB` [builder](#rune-0016)               | stub        | An order-word; it constrains how complexity is arranged rather than merely accumulated.                                                                                                 |
| 0017  | `STUB` [bus {Arch}](#rune-0017)            | stub        | A composition-word; it lets many smaller parts behave as one larger form.                                                                                                               |
| 0018  | `STUB` [capability {Arch}](#rune-0018)     | stub        | A composition-word; it lets many smaller parts behave as one larger form.                                                                                                               |
| 0019  | `STUB` [channel](#rune-0019)               | stub        | A boundary-word; it shapes how one part may touch another.                                                                                                                              |
| 0020  | `STUB` [client {Arch}](#rune-0020)         | stub        | An order-word; it constrains how complexity is arranged rather than merely accumulated.                                                                                                 |
| 0021  | `STUB` [cluster {Arch}](#rune-0021)        | stub        | A many-body word; several machines are treated as one operational unit.                                                                                                                 |
| 0022  | `STUB` [command bus](#rune-0022)           | stub        | A boundary-word; it shapes how one part may touch another.                                                                                                                              |
| 0023  | [component {Arch}](#rune-0023)             | authored    | A bounded unit of responsibility inside a larger design.                                                                                                                                |
| 0024  | `STUB` [composition](#rune-0024)           | stub        | A structure-word; it gives responsibility a place to live.                                                                                                                              |
| 0025  | `STUB` [connector](#rune-0025)             | stub        | An order-word; it constrains how complexity is arranged rather than merely accumulated.                                                                                                 |
| 0026  | `STUB` [container {Arch}](#rune-0026)      | stub        | An order-word; it constrains how complexity is arranged rather than merely accumulated.                                                                                                 |
| 0027  | `STUB` [control plane {Arch}](#rune-0027)  | stub        | A boundary-word; it shapes how one part may touch another.                                                                                                                              |
| 0028  | `STUB` [coordinator](#rune-0028)           | stub        | A structure-word; it gives responsibility a place to live.                                                                                                                              |
| 0029  | `STUB` [core {Arch}](#rune-0029)           | stub        | A boundary-word; it shapes how one part may touch another.                                                                                                                              |
| 0030  | [coupling](#rune-0030)                     | authored    | The hidden tax paid when change in one place forces change elsewhere. Name it early if you want modularity to survive contact with reality.                                             |
| 0031  | `STUB` [daemon {Arch}](#rune-0031)         | stub        | A structure-word; it gives responsibility a place to live.                                                                                                                              |
| 0032  | `STUB` [data plane {Arch}](#rune-0032)     | stub        | A composition-word; it lets many smaller parts behave as one larger form.                                                                                                               |
| 0033  | `STUB` [decorator {Arch}](#rune-0033)      | stub        | An order-word; it constrains how complexity is arranged rather than merely accumulated.                                                                                                 |
| 0034  | [dependency inversion](#rune-0034)         | authored    | Stable policy should not kneel before unstable detail. Shadow: concrete dependencies hard-wired into places that should remain portable.                                                |
| 0035  | `STUB` [dispatcher](#rune-0035)            | stub        | An order-word; it constrains how complexity is arranged rather than merely accumulated.                                                                                                 |
| 0036  | [domain model](#rune-0036)                 | authored    | The conceptual map of the business world the software is trying to serve.                                                                                                               |
| 0037  | `STUB` [edge service](#rune-0037)          | stub        | A composition-word; it lets many smaller parts behave as one larger form.                                                                                                               |
| 0038  | `STUB` [engine](#rune-0038)                | stub        | A composition-word; it lets many smaller parts behave as one larger form.                                                                                                               |
| 0039  | `STUB` [entrypoint](#rune-0039)            | stub        | A structure-word; it gives responsibility a place to live.                                                                                                                              |
| 0040  | `STUB` [event bus](#rune-0040)             | stub        | A structure-word; it gives responsibility a place to live.                                                                                                                              |
| 0041  | `STUB` [event handler](#rune-0041)         | stub        | A structure-word; it gives responsibility a place to live.                                                                                                                              |
| 0042  | `STUB` [executor](#rune-0042)              | stub        | An order-word; it constrains how complexity is arranged rather than merely accumulated.                                                                                                 |
| 0043  | [facade](#rune-0043)                       | authored    | A simplified front door hiding a messier interior.                                                                                                                                      |
| 0044  | `STUB` [factory](#rune-0044)               | stub        | A structure-word; it gives responsibility a place to live.                                                                                                                              |
| 0045  | `STUB` [feature module](#rune-0045)        | stub        | A composition-word; it lets many smaller parts behave as one larger form.                                                                                                               |
| 0046  | `STUB` [frontend](#rune-0046)              | stub        | A structure-word; it gives responsibility a place to live.                                                                                                                              |
| 0047  | `STUB` [gateway {Arch}](#rune-0047)        | stub        | A composition-word; it lets many smaller parts behave as one larger form.                                                                                                               |
| 0048  | `STUB` [graph](#rune-0048)                 | stub        | A composition-word; it lets many smaller parts behave as one larger form.                                                                                                               |
| 0049  | `STUB` [handler](#rune-0049)               | stub        | A composition-word; it lets many smaller parts behave as one larger form.                                                                                                               |
| 0050  | `STUB` [hierarchy {Arch}](#rune-0050)      | stub        | An order-word; it constrains how complexity is arranged rather than merely accumulated.                                                                                                 |
| 0051  | `STUB` [hook](#rune-0051)                  | stub        | A boundary-word; it shapes how one part may touch another.                                                                                                                              |
| 0052  | `STUB` [host](#rune-0052)                  | stub        | A composition-word; it lets many smaller parts behave as one larger form.                                                                                                               |
| 0053  | `STUB` [hub](#rune-0053)                   | stub        | An order-word; it constrains how complexity is arranged rather than merely accumulated.                                                                                                 |
| 0054  | `STUB` [implementation {Arch}](#rune-0054) | stub        | A composition-word; it lets many smaller parts behave as one larger form.                                                                                                               |
| 0055  | [interface {Arch}](#rune-0055)             | authored    | A disciplined seam that lets unlike bodies cooperate without swapping internal organs. Shadow: tight coupling and type bleed.                                                           |
| 0056  | `STUB` [interpreter {Arch}](#rune-0056)    | stub        | A structure-word; it gives responsibility a place to live.                                                                                                                              |
| 0057  | `STUB` [kernel service](#rune-0057)        | stub        | A composition-word; it lets many smaller parts behave as one larger form.                                                                                                               |
| 0058  | [layer](#rune-0058)                        | authored    | A stratum with a narrow job and limited allowed dependencies.                                                                                                                           |
| 0059  | `STUB` [library](#rune-0059)               | stub        | A boundary-word; it shapes how one part may touch another.                                                                                                                              |
| 0060  | `STUB` [listener](#rune-0060)              | stub        | A composition-word; it lets many smaller parts behave as one larger form.                                                                                                               |
| 0061  | `STUB` [machine boundary](#rune-0061)      | stub        | A boundary-word; it shapes how one part may touch another.                                                                                                                              |
| 0062  | `STUB` [mediator](#rune-0062)              | stub        | A boundary-word; it shapes how one part may touch another.                                                                                                                              |
| 0063  | `STUB` [mesh {Arch}](#rune-0063)           | stub        | A boundary-word; it shapes how one part may touch another.                                                                                                                              |
| 0064  | [microservice](#rune-0064)                 | needs_sense | A partition word; a system is cut into independently deployed service bodies.                                                                                                           |
| 0065  | [middleware](#rune-0065)                   | authored    | Cross-cutting logic placed in the path between request and handler.                                                                                                                     |
| 0066  | [module {Arch}](#rune-0066)                | authored    | A package of related definitions meant to travel as one thing.                                                                                                                          |
| 0067  | `STUB` [monolith](#rune-0067)              | stub        | A composition-word; it lets many smaller parts behave as one larger form.                                                                                                               |
| 0068  | `STUB` [namespace {Arch}](#rune-0068)      | stub        | A boundary-word; it shapes how one part may touch another.                                                                                                                              |
| 0069  | `STUB` [node {Arch}](#rune-0069)           | stub        | A composition-word; it lets many smaller parts behave as one larger form.                                                                                                               |
| 0070  | `STUB` [observer](#rune-0070)              | stub        | A structure-word; it gives responsibility a place to live.                                                                                                                              |
| 0071  | [orchestrator](#rune-0071)                 | authored    | A coordinator that sequences many services or tasks into one larger procedure.                                                                                                          |
| 0072  | `STUB` [package {Arch}](#rune-0072)        | stub        | A structure-word; it gives responsibility a place to live.                                                                                                                              |
| 0073  | `STUB` [pipeline {Arch}](#rune-0073)       | stub        | A procession word; work passes through ordered stages.                                                                                                                                  |
| 0074  | `STUB` [platform](#rune-0074)              | stub        | A boundary-word; it shapes how one part may touch another.                                                                                                                              |
| 0075  | [plugin](#rune-0075)                       | authored    | An extension point that lets capability be added without rewriting the host.                                                                                                            |
| 0076  | `STUB` [port {Arch}](#rune-0076)           | stub        | A contact word; a defined point of communication or adaptation.                                                                                                                         |
| 0077  | `STUB` [presenter](#rune-0077)             | stub        | A structure-word; it gives responsibility a place to live.                                                                                                                              |
| 0078  | `STUB` [process manager](#rune-0078)       | stub        | A composition-word; it lets many smaller parts behave as one larger form.                                                                                                               |
| 0079  | `STUB` [producer](#rune-0079)              | stub        | A structure-word; it gives responsibility a place to live.                                                                                                                              |
| 0080  | `STUB` [projection](#rune-0080)            | stub        | A boundary-word; it shapes how one part may touch another.                                                                                                                              |
| 0081  | `STUB` [protocol boundary](#rune-0081)     | stub        | A composition-word; it lets many smaller parts behave as one larger form.                                                                                                               |
| 0082  | [proxy {Arch}](#rune-0082)                 | authored    | A stand-in that speaks or receives on behalf of another body.                                                                                                                           |
| 0083  | `STUB` [queue {Arch}](#rune-0083)          | stub        | A boundary-word; it shapes how one part may touch another.                                                                                                                              |
| 0084  | `STUB` [reactor](#rune-0084)               | stub        | A structure-word; it gives responsibility a place to live.                                                                                                                              |
| 0085  | `STUB` [receiver](#rune-0085)              | stub        | A boundary-word; it shapes how one part may touch another.                                                                                                                              |
| 0086  | `STUB` [registry](#rune-0086)              | stub        | A boundary-word; it shapes how one part may touch another.                                                                                                                              |
| 0087  | `STUB` [repository {Arch}](#rune-0087)     | stub        | A custody word; an official place from which versions or entities are retrieved.                                                                                                        |
| 0088  | `STUB` [resource boundary](#rune-0088)     | stub        | A structure-word; it gives responsibility a place to live.                                                                                                                              |
| 0089  | `STUB` [router {Arch}](#rune-0089)         | stub        | A boundary-word; it shapes how one part may touch another.                                                                                                                              |
| 0090  | [scheduler](#rune-0090)                    | needs_sense | A tempo word; it apportions time among contenders.                                                                                                                                      |
| 0091  | `STUB` [seam](#rune-0091)                  | stub        | A composition-word; it lets many smaller parts behave as one larger form.                                                                                                               |
| 0092  | [service](#rune-0092)                      | authored    | A stable capability exposed for repeated use by others.                                                                                                                                 |
| 0093  | `STUB` [service mesh {Arch}](#rune-0093)   | stub        | A side-channel governance word for service-to-service traffic.                                                                                                                          |
| 0094  | `STUB` [session boundary](#rune-0094)      | stub        | A structure-word; it gives responsibility a place to live.                                                                                                                              |
| 0095  | `STUB` [shard {Arch}](#rune-0095)          | stub        | A splitting word; one logical body is divided across multiple partitions.                                                                                                               |
| 0096  | `STUB` [shim](#rune-0096)                  | stub        | A boundary-word; it shapes how one part may touch another.                                                                                                                              |
| 0097  | `STUB` [singleton](#rune-0097)             | stub        | An order-word; it constrains how complexity is arranged rather than merely accumulated.                                                                                                 |
| 0098  | [source of truth](#rune-0098)              | authored    | The place other copies are supposed to agree with.                                                                                                                                      |
| 0099  | `STUB` [stack {Arch}](#rune-0099)          | stub        | A layering word; order matters and the most recent thing is nearest the top.                                                                                                            |
| 0100  | `STUB` [stage {Arch}](#rune-0100)          | stub        | A structure-word; it gives responsibility a place to live.                                                                                                                              |
| 0101  | [state machine](#rune-0101)                | authored    | Behavior expressed as named states and legal transitions.                                                                                                                               |
| 0102  | `STUB` [strategy](#rune-0102)              | stub        | An order-word; it constrains how complexity is arranged rather than merely accumulated.                                                                                                 |
| 0103  | `STUB` [subsystem](#rune-0103)             | stub        | An order-word; it constrains how complexity is arranged rather than merely accumulated.                                                                                                 |
| 0104  | `STUB` [surface](#rune-0104)               | stub        | A boundary-word; it shapes how one part may touch another.                                                                                                                              |
| 0105  | `STUB` [system](#rune-0105)                | stub        | A boundary-word; it shapes how one part may touch another.                                                                                                                              |
| 0106  | `STUB` [template method](#rune-0106)       | stub        | A boundary-word; it shapes how one part may touch another.                                                                                                                              |
| 0107  | `STUB` [tree](#rune-0107)                  | stub        | A structure-word; it gives responsibility a place to live.                                                                                                                              |
| 0108  | `STUB` [use case](#rune-0108)              | stub        | A boundary-word; it shapes how one part may touch another.                                                                                                                              |
| 0109  | `STUB` [value object](#rune-0109)          | stub        | A structure-word; it gives responsibility a place to live.                                                                                                                              |
| 0110  | [worker](#rune-0110)                       | needs_sense | A labor word; a designated body performs tasks delegated by some larger system.                                                                                                         |
| 0111  | `STUB` [workflow](#rune-0111)              | stub        | An order-word; it constrains how complexity is arranged rather than merely accumulated.                                                                                                 |
| 0112  | `STUB` [wrapper](#rune-0112)               | stub        | A boundary-word; it shapes how one part may touch another.                                                                                                                              |

## Entries

### [0001] abstraction {#rune-0001}

**Status:** canonical (major canon, pocket canon)

**Completion:** authored

**Force:** A deliberate forgetting of detail so larger structure can be reasoned about without drowning in mechanism.

**Shadow:** hiding the only failure that matters.

**Pocket gloss:** Deliberate forgetting of detail so larger structure can be reasoned about cleanly.

### [0002] adapter {#rune-0002}

**Status:** canonical (major canon, pocket canon)

**Completion:** authored

**Force:** A negotiated translator between unlike shapes. Use it when two systems must meet without infecting one another.

**Shadow:** adapter jungles that preserve confusion rather than resolve it.

**Pocket gloss:** A translator between unlike shapes that lets systems meet without infecting one another.

### [0003] agent {Arch} {#rune-0003}

**Status:** canonical

**Completion:** stub

**Force:** An acting word; a named locus of initiative that may observe, decide, and invoke tools.

**Shadow:** the boundary or abstraction can hide the failure that actually needs ownership.

### [0004] aggregate {Arch} {#rune-0004}

**Status:** canonical

**Completion:** stub

**Force:** An order-word; it constrains how complexity is arranged rather than merely accumulated.

**Shadow:** the boundary or abstraction can hide the failure that actually needs ownership.

### [0005] anti-corruption layer {#rune-0005}

**Status:** canonical (pocket canon)

**Completion:** authored

**Force:** A protective seam that keeps one model from polluting another.

**Shadow:** the boundary or abstraction can hide the failure that actually needs ownership.

**Pocket gloss:** A protective seam that keeps one model from polluting another.

### [0006] API gateway {#rune-0006}

**Status:** canonical (major canon, pocket canon)

**Completion:** authored

**Force:** A threshold that centralizes entry, policy, and routing at the edge of a service world.

**Shadow:** a convenience chokepoint that quietly becomes a monolith.

**Pocket gloss:** An edge threshold that centralizes entry, policy, and routing.

### [0007] architecture {#rune-0007}

**Status:** canonical

**Completion:** needs_sense

**Force:** A world-shaping word; it names the large order into which many smaller decisions must fit.

**Shadow:** the boundary or abstraction can hide the failure that actually needs ownership.

### [0008] artifact boundary {#rune-0008}

**Status:** canonical

**Completion:** stub

**Force:** A composition-word; it lets many smaller parts behave as one larger form.

**Shadow:** the boundary or abstraction can hide the failure that actually needs ownership.

### [0009] assembler {#rune-0009}

**Status:** canonical

**Completion:** stub

**Force:** A composition-word; it lets many smaller parts behave as one larger form.

**Shadow:** the boundary or abstraction can hide the failure that actually needs ownership.

### [0010] backbone {#rune-0010}

**Status:** canonical

**Completion:** stub

**Force:** A boundary-word; it shapes how one part may touch another.

**Shadow:** the boundary or abstraction can hide the failure that actually needs ownership.

### [0011] backend {#rune-0011}

**Status:** canonical

**Completion:** stub

**Force:** A structure-word; it gives responsibility a place to live.

**Shadow:** the boundary or abstraction can hide the failure that actually needs ownership.

### [0012] boundary {#rune-0012}

**Status:** canonical (major canon, pocket canon)

**Completion:** authored

**Force:** The line that says where one responsibility ends and another begins.

**Shadow:** porous edges that smear blame, ownership, and test scope.

**Pocket gloss:** The line where one responsibility ends and another begins.

### [0013] bounded context {#rune-0013}

**Status:** canonical (major canon, pocket canon)

**Completion:** authored

**Force:** A protection against semantic bleed: the same word may not mean the same thing in every subsystem.

**Shadow:** false unification through shared names.

**Pocket gloss:** A semantic fence: the same term is not forced to mean the same thing everywhere.

### [0014] bridge {#rune-0014}

**Status:** canonical

**Completion:** stub

**Force:** A boundary-word; it shapes how one part may touch another.

**Shadow:** the boundary or abstraction can hide the failure that actually needs ownership.

### [0015] broker {Arch} {#rune-0015}

**Status:** canonical

**Completion:** stub

**Force:** A boundary-word; it shapes how one part may touch another.

**Shadow:** the boundary or abstraction can hide the failure that actually needs ownership.

### [0016] builder {#rune-0016}

**Status:** canonical

**Completion:** stub

**Force:** An order-word; it constrains how complexity is arranged rather than merely accumulated.

**Shadow:** the boundary or abstraction can hide the failure that actually needs ownership.

### [0017] bus {Arch} {#rune-0017}

**Status:** canonical

**Completion:** stub

**Force:** A composition-word; it lets many smaller parts behave as one larger form.

**Shadow:** the boundary or abstraction can hide the failure that actually needs ownership.

### [0018] capability {Arch} {#rune-0018}

**Status:** canonical

**Completion:** stub

**Force:** A composition-word; it lets many smaller parts behave as one larger form.

**Shadow:** the boundary or abstraction can hide the failure that actually needs ownership.

### [0019] channel {#rune-0019}

**Status:** canonical

**Completion:** stub

**Force:** A boundary-word; it shapes how one part may touch another.

**Shadow:** the boundary or abstraction can hide the failure that actually needs ownership.

### [0020] client {Arch} {#rune-0020}

**Status:** canonical

**Completion:** stub

**Force:** An order-word; it constrains how complexity is arranged rather than merely accumulated.

**Shadow:** the boundary or abstraction can hide the failure that actually needs ownership.

### [0021] cluster {Arch} {#rune-0021}

**Status:** canonical

**Completion:** stub

**Force:** A many-body word; several machines are treated as one operational unit.

**Shadow:** the boundary or abstraction can hide the failure that actually needs ownership.

### [0022] command bus {#rune-0022}

**Status:** canonical

**Completion:** stub

**Force:** A boundary-word; it shapes how one part may touch another.

**Shadow:** the boundary or abstraction can hide the failure that actually needs ownership.

### [0023] component {Arch} {#rune-0023}

**Status:** canonical (pocket canon)

**Completion:** authored

**Force:** A bounded unit of responsibility inside a larger design.

**Shadow:** the boundary or abstraction can hide the failure that actually needs ownership.

**Pocket gloss:** A bounded unit of responsibility inside a larger design.

### [0024] composition {#rune-0024}

**Status:** canonical

**Completion:** stub

**Force:** A structure-word; it gives responsibility a place to live.

**Shadow:** the boundary or abstraction can hide the failure that actually needs ownership.

### [0025] connector {#rune-0025}

**Status:** canonical

**Completion:** stub

**Force:** An order-word; it constrains how complexity is arranged rather than merely accumulated.

**Shadow:** the boundary or abstraction can hide the failure that actually needs ownership.

### [0026] container {Arch} {#rune-0026}

**Status:** canonical

**Completion:** stub

**Force:** An order-word; it constrains how complexity is arranged rather than merely accumulated.

**Shadow:** the boundary or abstraction can hide the failure that actually needs ownership.

### [0027] control plane {Arch} {#rune-0027}

**Status:** canonical

**Completion:** stub

**Force:** A boundary-word; it shapes how one part may touch another.

**Shadow:** the boundary or abstraction can hide the failure that actually needs ownership.

### [0028] coordinator {#rune-0028}

**Status:** canonical

**Completion:** stub

**Force:** A structure-word; it gives responsibility a place to live.

**Shadow:** the boundary or abstraction can hide the failure that actually needs ownership.

### [0029] core {Arch} {#rune-0029}

**Status:** canonical

**Completion:** stub

**Force:** A boundary-word; it shapes how one part may touch another.

**Shadow:** the boundary or abstraction can hide the failure that actually needs ownership.

### [0030] coupling {#rune-0030}

**Status:** canonical (major canon, pocket canon)

**Completion:** authored

**Force:** The hidden tax paid when change in one place forces change elsewhere. Name it early if you want modularity to survive contact with reality.

**Shadow:** when a change in one component quietly forces coordinated edits across unrelated surfaces.

**Pocket gloss:** The hidden tax paid when change in one place forces change elsewhere.

### [0031] daemon {Arch} {#rune-0031}

**Status:** canonical

**Completion:** stub

**Force:** A structure-word; it gives responsibility a place to live.

**Shadow:** the boundary or abstraction can hide the failure that actually needs ownership.

### [0032] data plane {Arch} {#rune-0032}

**Status:** canonical

**Completion:** stub

**Force:** A composition-word; it lets many smaller parts behave as one larger form.

**Shadow:** the boundary or abstraction can hide the failure that actually needs ownership.

### [0033] decorator {Arch} {#rune-0033}

**Status:** canonical

**Completion:** stub

**Force:** An order-word; it constrains how complexity is arranged rather than merely accumulated.

**Shadow:** the boundary or abstraction can hide the failure that actually needs ownership.

### [0034] dependency inversion {#rune-0034}

**Status:** canonical (major canon, pocket canon)

**Completion:** authored

**Force:** Stable policy should not kneel before unstable detail.

**Shadow:** concrete dependencies hard-wired into places that should remain portable.

**Pocket gloss:** Stable policy should not kneel before unstable detail.

### [0035] dispatcher {#rune-0035}

**Status:** canonical

**Completion:** stub

**Force:** An order-word; it constrains how complexity is arranged rather than merely accumulated.

**Shadow:** the boundary or abstraction can hide the failure that actually needs ownership.

### [0036] domain model {#rune-0036}

**Status:** canonical (pocket canon)

**Completion:** authored

**Force:** The conceptual map of the business world the software is trying to serve.

**Shadow:** the boundary or abstraction can hide the failure that actually needs ownership.

**Pocket gloss:** The conceptual map of the business world the software is trying to serve.

### [0037] edge service {#rune-0037}

**Status:** canonical

**Completion:** stub

**Force:** A composition-word; it lets many smaller parts behave as one larger form.

**Shadow:** the boundary or abstraction can hide the failure that actually needs ownership.

### [0038] engine {#rune-0038}

**Status:** canonical

**Completion:** stub

**Force:** A composition-word; it lets many smaller parts behave as one larger form.

**Shadow:** the boundary or abstraction can hide the failure that actually needs ownership.

### [0039] entrypoint {#rune-0039}

**Status:** canonical

**Completion:** stub

**Force:** A structure-word; it gives responsibility a place to live.

**Shadow:** the boundary or abstraction can hide the failure that actually needs ownership.

### [0040] event bus {#rune-0040}

**Status:** canonical

**Completion:** stub

**Force:** A structure-word; it gives responsibility a place to live.

**Shadow:** the boundary or abstraction can hide the failure that actually needs ownership.

### [0041] event handler {#rune-0041}

**Status:** canonical

**Completion:** stub

**Force:** A structure-word; it gives responsibility a place to live.

**Shadow:** the boundary or abstraction can hide the failure that actually needs ownership.

### [0042] executor {#rune-0042}

**Status:** canonical

**Completion:** stub

**Force:** An order-word; it constrains how complexity is arranged rather than merely accumulated.

**Shadow:** the boundary or abstraction can hide the failure that actually needs ownership.

### [0043] facade {#rune-0043}

**Status:** canonical (pocket canon)

**Completion:** authored

**Force:** A simplified front door hiding a messier interior.

**Shadow:** the boundary or abstraction can hide the failure that actually needs ownership.

**Pocket gloss:** A simplified front door hiding a messier interior.

### [0044] factory {#rune-0044}

**Status:** canonical

**Completion:** stub

**Force:** A structure-word; it gives responsibility a place to live.

**Shadow:** the boundary or abstraction can hide the failure that actually needs ownership.

### [0045] feature module {#rune-0045}

**Status:** canonical

**Completion:** stub

**Force:** A composition-word; it lets many smaller parts behave as one larger form.

**Shadow:** the boundary or abstraction can hide the failure that actually needs ownership.

### [0046] frontend {#rune-0046}

**Status:** canonical

**Completion:** stub

**Force:** A structure-word; it gives responsibility a place to live.

**Shadow:** the boundary or abstraction can hide the failure that actually needs ownership.

### [0047] gateway {Arch} {#rune-0047}

**Status:** canonical

**Completion:** stub

**Force:** A composition-word; it lets many smaller parts behave as one larger form.

**Shadow:** the boundary or abstraction can hide the failure that actually needs ownership.

### [0048] graph {#rune-0048}

**Status:** canonical

**Completion:** stub

**Force:** A composition-word; it lets many smaller parts behave as one larger form.

**Shadow:** the boundary or abstraction can hide the failure that actually needs ownership.

### [0049] handler {#rune-0049}

**Status:** canonical

**Completion:** stub

**Force:** A composition-word; it lets many smaller parts behave as one larger form.

**Shadow:** the boundary or abstraction can hide the failure that actually needs ownership.

### [0050] hierarchy {Arch} {#rune-0050}

**Status:** canonical

**Completion:** stub

**Force:** An order-word; it constrains how complexity is arranged rather than merely accumulated.

**Shadow:** the boundary or abstraction can hide the failure that actually needs ownership.

### [0051] hook {#rune-0051}

**Status:** canonical

**Completion:** stub

**Force:** A boundary-word; it shapes how one part may touch another.

**Shadow:** the boundary or abstraction can hide the failure that actually needs ownership.

### [0052] host {#rune-0052}

**Status:** canonical

**Completion:** stub

**Force:** A composition-word; it lets many smaller parts behave as one larger form.

**Shadow:** the boundary or abstraction can hide the failure that actually needs ownership.

### [0053] hub {#rune-0053}

**Status:** canonical

**Completion:** stub

**Force:** An order-word; it constrains how complexity is arranged rather than merely accumulated.

**Shadow:** the boundary or abstraction can hide the failure that actually needs ownership.

### [0054] implementation {Arch} {#rune-0054}

**Status:** canonical

**Completion:** stub

**Force:** A composition-word; it lets many smaller parts behave as one larger form.

**Shadow:** the boundary or abstraction can hide the failure that actually needs ownership.

### [0055] interface {Arch} {#rune-0055}

**Status:** canonical (major canon, pocket canon)

**Completion:** authored

**Force:** A disciplined seam that lets unlike bodies cooperate without swapping internal organs.

**Shadow:** tight coupling and type bleed.

**Pocket gloss:** A disciplined seam that lets unlike bodies cooperate without swapping internal organs.

### [0056] interpreter {Arch} {#rune-0056}

**Status:** canonical

**Completion:** stub

**Force:** A structure-word; it gives responsibility a place to live.

**Shadow:** the boundary or abstraction can hide the failure that actually needs ownership.

### [0057] kernel service {#rune-0057}

**Status:** canonical

**Completion:** stub

**Force:** A composition-word; it lets many smaller parts behave as one larger form.

**Shadow:** the boundary or abstraction can hide the failure that actually needs ownership.

### [0058] layer {#rune-0058}

**Status:** canonical (pocket canon)

**Completion:** authored

**Force:** A stratum with a narrow job and limited allowed dependencies.

**Shadow:** the boundary or abstraction can hide the failure that actually needs ownership.

**Pocket gloss:** A stratum with a narrow job and limited allowed dependencies.

### [0059] library {#rune-0059}

**Status:** canonical

**Completion:** stub

**Force:** A boundary-word; it shapes how one part may touch another.

**Shadow:** the boundary or abstraction can hide the failure that actually needs ownership.

### [0060] listener {#rune-0060}

**Status:** canonical

**Completion:** stub

**Force:** A composition-word; it lets many smaller parts behave as one larger form.

**Shadow:** the boundary or abstraction can hide the failure that actually needs ownership.

### [0061] machine boundary {#rune-0061}

**Status:** canonical

**Completion:** stub

**Force:** A boundary-word; it shapes how one part may touch another.

**Shadow:** the boundary or abstraction can hide the failure that actually needs ownership.

### [0062] mediator {#rune-0062}

**Status:** canonical

**Completion:** stub

**Force:** A boundary-word; it shapes how one part may touch another.

**Shadow:** the boundary or abstraction can hide the failure that actually needs ownership.

### [0063] mesh {Arch} {#rune-0063}

**Status:** canonical

**Completion:** stub

**Force:** A boundary-word; it shapes how one part may touch another.

**Shadow:** the boundary or abstraction can hide the failure that actually needs ownership.

### [0064] microservice {#rune-0064}

**Status:** canonical

**Completion:** needs_sense

**Force:** A partition word; a system is cut into independently deployed service bodies.

**Shadow:** the boundary or abstraction can hide the failure that actually needs ownership.

### [0065] middleware {#rune-0065}

**Status:** canonical (pocket canon)

**Completion:** authored

**Force:** Cross-cutting logic placed in the path between request and handler.

**Shadow:** the boundary or abstraction can hide the failure that actually needs ownership.

**Pocket gloss:** Cross-cutting logic placed in the path between request and handler.

### [0066] module {Arch} {#rune-0066}

**Status:** canonical (pocket canon)

**Completion:** authored

**Force:** A package of related definitions meant to travel as one thing.

**Shadow:** the boundary or abstraction can hide the failure that actually needs ownership.

**Pocket gloss:** A package of related definitions meant to travel as one thing.

### [0067] monolith {#rune-0067}

**Status:** canonical

**Completion:** stub

**Force:** A composition-word; it lets many smaller parts behave as one larger form.

**Shadow:** the boundary or abstraction can hide the failure that actually needs ownership.

### [0068] namespace {Arch} {#rune-0068}

**Status:** canonical

**Completion:** stub

**Force:** A boundary-word; it shapes how one part may touch another.

**Shadow:** the boundary or abstraction can hide the failure that actually needs ownership.

### [0069] node {Arch} {#rune-0069}

**Status:** canonical

**Completion:** stub

**Force:** A composition-word; it lets many smaller parts behave as one larger form.

**Shadow:** the boundary or abstraction can hide the failure that actually needs ownership.

### [0070] observer {#rune-0070}

**Status:** canonical

**Completion:** stub

**Force:** A structure-word; it gives responsibility a place to live.

**Shadow:** the boundary or abstraction can hide the failure that actually needs ownership.

### [0071] orchestrator {#rune-0071}

**Status:** canonical (pocket canon)

**Completion:** authored

**Force:** A coordinator that sequences many services or tasks into one larger procedure.

**Shadow:** the boundary or abstraction can hide the failure that actually needs ownership.

**Pocket gloss:** A coordinator that sequences many services or tasks into one larger procedure.

### [0072] package {Arch} {#rune-0072}

**Status:** canonical

**Completion:** stub

**Force:** A structure-word; it gives responsibility a place to live.

**Shadow:** the boundary or abstraction can hide the failure that actually needs ownership.

### [0073] pipeline {Arch} {#rune-0073}

**Status:** canonical

**Completion:** stub

**Force:** A procession word; work passes through ordered stages.

**Shadow:** the boundary or abstraction can hide the failure that actually needs ownership.

### [0074] platform {#rune-0074}

**Status:** canonical

**Completion:** stub

**Force:** A boundary-word; it shapes how one part may touch another.

**Shadow:** the boundary or abstraction can hide the failure that actually needs ownership.

### [0075] plugin {#rune-0075}

**Status:** canonical (pocket canon)

**Completion:** authored

**Force:** An extension point that lets capability be added without rewriting the host.

**Shadow:** the boundary or abstraction can hide the failure that actually needs ownership.

**Pocket gloss:** An extension point that lets capability be added without rewriting the host.

### [0076] port {Arch} {#rune-0076}

**Status:** canonical

**Completion:** stub

**Force:** A contact word; a defined point of communication or adaptation.

**Shadow:** the boundary or abstraction can hide the failure that actually needs ownership.

### [0077] presenter {#rune-0077}

**Status:** canonical

**Completion:** stub

**Force:** A structure-word; it gives responsibility a place to live.

**Shadow:** the boundary or abstraction can hide the failure that actually needs ownership.

### [0078] process manager {#rune-0078}

**Status:** canonical

**Completion:** stub

**Force:** A composition-word; it lets many smaller parts behave as one larger form.

**Shadow:** the boundary or abstraction can hide the failure that actually needs ownership.

### [0079] producer {#rune-0079}

**Status:** canonical

**Completion:** stub

**Force:** A structure-word; it gives responsibility a place to live.

**Shadow:** the boundary or abstraction can hide the failure that actually needs ownership.

### [0080] projection {#rune-0080}

**Status:** canonical

**Completion:** stub

**Force:** A boundary-word; it shapes how one part may touch another.

**Shadow:** the boundary or abstraction can hide the failure that actually needs ownership.

### [0081] protocol boundary {#rune-0081}

**Status:** canonical

**Completion:** stub

**Force:** A composition-word; it lets many smaller parts behave as one larger form.

**Shadow:** the boundary or abstraction can hide the failure that actually needs ownership.

### [0082] proxy {Arch} {#rune-0082}

**Status:** canonical (pocket canon)

**Completion:** authored

**Force:** A stand-in that speaks or receives on behalf of another body.

**Shadow:** the boundary or abstraction can hide the failure that actually needs ownership.

**Pocket gloss:** A stand-in that speaks or receives on behalf of another body.

### [0083] queue {Arch} {#rune-0083}

**Status:** canonical

**Completion:** stub

**Force:** A boundary-word; it shapes how one part may touch another.

**Shadow:** the boundary or abstraction can hide the failure that actually needs ownership.

### [0084] reactor {#rune-0084}

**Status:** canonical

**Completion:** stub

**Force:** A structure-word; it gives responsibility a place to live.

**Shadow:** the boundary or abstraction can hide the failure that actually needs ownership.

### [0085] receiver {#rune-0085}

**Status:** canonical

**Completion:** stub

**Force:** A boundary-word; it shapes how one part may touch another.

**Shadow:** the boundary or abstraction can hide the failure that actually needs ownership.

### [0086] registry {#rune-0086}

**Status:** canonical

**Completion:** stub

**Force:** A boundary-word; it shapes how one part may touch another.

**Shadow:** the boundary or abstraction can hide the failure that actually needs ownership.

### [0087] repository {Arch} {#rune-0087}

**Status:** canonical

**Completion:** stub

**Force:** A custody word; an official place from which versions or entities are retrieved.

**Shadow:** the boundary or abstraction can hide the failure that actually needs ownership.

### [0088] resource boundary {#rune-0088}

**Status:** canonical

**Completion:** stub

**Force:** A structure-word; it gives responsibility a place to live.

**Shadow:** the boundary or abstraction can hide the failure that actually needs ownership.

### [0089] router {Arch} {#rune-0089}

**Status:** canonical

**Completion:** stub

**Force:** A boundary-word; it shapes how one part may touch another.

**Shadow:** the boundary or abstraction can hide the failure that actually needs ownership.

### [0090] scheduler {#rune-0090}

**Status:** canonical

**Completion:** needs_sense

**Force:** A tempo word; it apportions time among contenders.

**Shadow:** the boundary or abstraction can hide the failure that actually needs ownership.

### [0091] seam {#rune-0091}

**Status:** canonical

**Completion:** stub

**Force:** A composition-word; it lets many smaller parts behave as one larger form.

**Shadow:** the boundary or abstraction can hide the failure that actually needs ownership.

### [0092] service {#rune-0092}

**Status:** canonical (pocket canon)

**Completion:** authored

**Force:** A stable capability exposed for repeated use by others.

**Shadow:** the boundary or abstraction can hide the failure that actually needs ownership.

**Pocket gloss:** A stable capability exposed for repeated use by others.

### [0093] service mesh {Arch} {#rune-0093}

**Status:** canonical

**Completion:** stub

**Force:** A side-channel governance word for service-to-service traffic.

**Shadow:** the boundary or abstraction can hide the failure that actually needs ownership.

### [0094] session boundary {#rune-0094}

**Status:** canonical

**Completion:** stub

**Force:** A structure-word; it gives responsibility a place to live.

**Shadow:** the boundary or abstraction can hide the failure that actually needs ownership.

### [0095] shard {Arch} {#rune-0095}

**Status:** canonical

**Completion:** stub

**Force:** A splitting word; one logical body is divided across multiple partitions.

**Shadow:** the boundary or abstraction can hide the failure that actually needs ownership.

### [0096] shim {#rune-0096}

**Status:** canonical

**Completion:** stub

**Force:** A boundary-word; it shapes how one part may touch another.

**Shadow:** the boundary or abstraction can hide the failure that actually needs ownership.

### [0097] singleton {#rune-0097}

**Status:** canonical

**Completion:** stub

**Force:** An order-word; it constrains how complexity is arranged rather than merely accumulated.

**Shadow:** the boundary or abstraction can hide the failure that actually needs ownership.

### [0098] source of truth {#rune-0098}

**Status:** canonical (pocket canon)

**Completion:** authored

**Force:** The place other copies are supposed to agree with.

**Shadow:** the boundary or abstraction can hide the failure that actually needs ownership.

**Pocket gloss:** The place other copies are supposed to agree with.

### [0099] stack {Arch} {#rune-0099}

**Status:** canonical

**Completion:** stub

**Force:** A layering word; order matters and the most recent thing is nearest the top.

**Shadow:** the boundary or abstraction can hide the failure that actually needs ownership.

### [0100] stage {Arch} {#rune-0100}

**Status:** canonical

**Completion:** stub

**Force:** A structure-word; it gives responsibility a place to live.

**Shadow:** the boundary or abstraction can hide the failure that actually needs ownership.

### [0101] state machine {#rune-0101}

**Status:** canonical (pocket canon)

**Completion:** authored

**Force:** Behavior expressed as named states and legal transitions.

**Shadow:** the boundary or abstraction can hide the failure that actually needs ownership.

**Pocket gloss:** Behavior expressed as named states and legal transitions.

### [0102] strategy {#rune-0102}

**Status:** canonical

**Completion:** stub

**Force:** An order-word; it constrains how complexity is arranged rather than merely accumulated.

**Shadow:** the boundary or abstraction can hide the failure that actually needs ownership.

### [0103] subsystem {#rune-0103}

**Status:** canonical

**Completion:** stub

**Force:** An order-word; it constrains how complexity is arranged rather than merely accumulated.

**Shadow:** the boundary or abstraction can hide the failure that actually needs ownership.

### [0104] surface {#rune-0104}

**Status:** canonical

**Completion:** stub

**Force:** A boundary-word; it shapes how one part may touch another.

**Shadow:** the boundary or abstraction can hide the failure that actually needs ownership.

### [0105] system {#rune-0105}

**Status:** canonical

**Completion:** stub

**Force:** A boundary-word; it shapes how one part may touch another.

**Shadow:** the boundary or abstraction can hide the failure that actually needs ownership.

### [0106] template method {#rune-0106}

**Status:** canonical

**Completion:** stub

**Force:** A boundary-word; it shapes how one part may touch another.

**Shadow:** the boundary or abstraction can hide the failure that actually needs ownership.

### [0107] tree {#rune-0107}

**Status:** canonical

**Completion:** stub

**Force:** A structure-word; it gives responsibility a place to live.

**Shadow:** the boundary or abstraction can hide the failure that actually needs ownership.

### [0108] use case {#rune-0108}

**Status:** canonical

**Completion:** stub

**Force:** A boundary-word; it shapes how one part may touch another.

**Shadow:** the boundary or abstraction can hide the failure that actually needs ownership.

### [0109] value object {#rune-0109}

**Status:** canonical

**Completion:** stub

**Force:** A structure-word; it gives responsibility a place to live.

**Shadow:** the boundary or abstraction can hide the failure that actually needs ownership.

### [0110] worker {#rune-0110}

**Status:** canonical

**Completion:** needs_sense

**Force:** A labor word; a designated body performs tasks delegated by some larger system.

**Shadow:** the boundary or abstraction can hide the failure that actually needs ownership.

### [0111] workflow {#rune-0111}

**Status:** canonical

**Completion:** stub

**Force:** An order-word; it constrains how complexity is arranged rather than merely accumulated.

**Shadow:** the boundary or abstraction can hide the failure that actually needs ownership.

### [0112] wrapper {#rune-0112}

**Status:** canonical

**Completion:** stub

**Force:** A boundary-word; it shapes how one part may touch another.

**Shadow:** the boundary or abstraction can hide the failure that actually needs ownership.
